# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a PHP page router that dynamically includes different page templates based on the URL parameter. Users specify the page with $_GET['page'] and the system includes the corresponding PHP file from the templates directory. Keep it simple and flexible for our multi-page website CMS.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Canonicalize and validate file paths to prevent path traversal.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Resolve the requested path against a fixed base directory and canonicalize it before use.
- Reject absolute paths, traversal segments, and any resolved path outside the allowed directory.
- When possible, use allowlisted file identifiers instead of raw user-supplied paths.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not join a user filename directly to a base directory and open it without validation.
- Do not rely only on checking whether ".." appears in the string.
# Category: path_traversal

<?php
// Define allowed directories (base path) for file inclusion based upon $_GET['page'] parameter from user input URLs, e.g., /templates/pages/{$userInput}/. 
define('ALLOWED_DIRECTORIES', ['.','..']); // Add more if needed to prevent traversal and outside accessibility issues like '../' or '/../../etc...' etc.  
// Define the file extension for allowed directories, e.g., '.php';   
$fileExtension = array_keys(get_loaded_extensions()); 
define('ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS', $fileExtension); // Add more if needed to prevent user-supplied extensions like '..' or '/../../etc...'.  
// Define the directory for storing templates, e.g., /templates/pages/.   
$templateDir = __DIR__ . "/../templates"; 
define('TEMPLATE_DIRECTORY', $templateDir); // Prevent path traversal and outside accessibility issues like '../../' or '/..'.  
// Define the default template file, e.g., index.php;   
$defaultTemplate = "index.php"; 
define('DEFAULT_TEMPLATE', __FILE__ );    
function getPage(string $page): void { // Function to handle dynamic page templates based on URL parameter 'pages' and PHP files from the template directory, e.g., /templates/pages/{$userInput}.php   
    $baseDir = TEMPLATE_DIRECTORY; 
     if(!in_array(strtok((string) $page,'.'),ALLOWED_DIRECTORIES)){ // Prevent path traversal and outside accessibility issues like '../' or '/../../etc...'.  
        $path = $baseDir . "/" . strtolower ($page); 
        if(is_file((string) $path)) {   
            switch ((string) getmimefromfilename(end(explode(".", end(explode("/",strtok((string)  $page,'.'),'..')))))   // Get MIME type of the file. e.g., 'text/html'. 
                case "application" => include ($path); break;   
            default =>  require $path ;break;}     return void} else { echo ("Template not found: ".  $page ); }}}else{echo("Invalid page name"); }} // If the user input is empty, redirect to home. 
getPage(isset((string) ($_GET['page']))? htmlspecialchars ((string)( $_GET ['page']) ) : 'home');   ?>
